title: sonchus × novocastellanus
intro: Sonchus × novocastellanus, also known as the Newcastle leek or Newcastle sow thistle, is a hybrid plant derived from the cross between Sonchus oleraceus (wild lettuce or sow thistle) and Sonchus arvensis (field sow thistle). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Sonchus × novocastellanus are attributed to its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Sonchus × novocastellanus are typically made from the plant's leaves and roots, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures.
